label: US President fires DHS Secretary Kristi Noem amid border policy friction
label_zh: 美國總統因邊境政策摩擦解僱國土安全部長克莉絲汀·諾姆

timeline.title: DHS Scuttles Luxury Jet Acquisition as Senate Sets Confirmation Hearing for Markwayne Mullin
timeline.title_zh: 特朗普政府就海地驅逐令封鎖提出上訴，國土安全部關閉進入第二個月
timeline.sub_title: Internal fallout from Kristi Noem’s firing widens with the cancellation of a high-profile aircraft purchase and a tentative date for her successor’s crucial testimony.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 在克里斯蒂·諾姆部長被解職後，政府尋求最高法院對關鍵邊境政策進行干預，同時持續的預算僵局導致數千名安保人員無薪工作。
timeline.summary:
The Department of Homeland Security moved to distance itself further from the tenure of ousted Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday, as internal reports confirmed the agency is reevaluating and likely canceling the purchase of a Boeing 737 MAX luxury jet. The aircraft, colloquially referred to by DHS staff as the “Big Beautiful Jet,” had become a symbol of the perceived mismanagement and excessive spending that contributed to Noem’s abrupt firing on March 5. Tensions regarding the jet reached a peak following revelations that Noem and her senior adviser, Corey Lewandowski, utilized the private cabin for travel that critics labeled as personal rather than operational. This latest move to scrap the acquisition reflects a broader effort within the administration to reset the department’s image ahead of new leadership.

Simultaneously, Senate Homeland Security Committee Chairman Rand Paul announced a tentative date of March 18 for the confirmation hearing of Senator Markwayne Mullin, whom President Trump has tapped to lead the agency. Mullin, an Oklahoma Republican and vocal supporter of the administration’s “America First” agenda, is expected to face intense questioning regarding his plans to stabilize a department currently mired in a partial funding shutdown. While some Republicans have signaled support for Mullin as a “steadier hand,” Senate Democrats have remained steadfast in their opposition to funding the agency until significant reforms are made to immigration enforcement tactics, including stricter oversight of the Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) division.

In a bid to alleviate the mounting public pressure caused by the DHS funding lapse, the administration reactivated the Global Entry program early Wednesday morning. The fast-track travel system had been suspended for weeks, leading to massive delays and hours-long security lines at major international airports. White House officials presented [ref:1] the reactivation as a necessary measure to protect the travel industry and regular citizens from the fallout of the congressional budget standoff. However, the move has done little to bridge the political divide, as House and Senate leaders continue to clash over the long-term direction of border policy and the use of federal agents in urban enforcement operations.

Outside of the administrative transitions, the department continues to grapple with the legal and ethical repercussions of Noem’s aggressive policies. New reports have emerged detailing an “unprecedented” attempt by DHS to gain access to a federal database intended for child support collection, ostensibly to track and deport undocumented immigrants. Legal experts and former officials from the Department of Health and Human Services have raised alarms that such a move could violate federal law. This controversy joins a growing list of challenges for the incoming secretary, including an investigation into senior border official Gregory Bovino for alleged misconduct and a record-setting pace of in-custody deaths within the migrant detention system.

For her part, Kristi Noem has officially transitioned into her new role as Special Envoy for the “Shield of the Americas.” Following a regional summit in Miami over the weekend, Noem is now tasked with coordinating a 13-nation alliance aimed at dismantling cartels and countering foreign influence in the Western Hemisphere. While she has publicly celebrated her new assignment, her departure from the Cabinet continues to reverberate through Washington as lawmakers and advocacy groups demand a full accounting of the operational failures that marked her thirteen months at the helm of the nation’s domestic security apparatus.
timeline.summary_zh:
國土安全部長克里斯蒂·諾姆被解職後，餘波週四加劇，特朗普政府向美國最高法院提起緊急上訴。總檢察長D.約翰·索爾週三晚間提交了一份請求，敦促大法官撤銷下級法院的禁令，該禁令阻止國土安全部終止對超過35萬名海地國民的臨時保護身份（TPS）。政府認為，司法部門正在越權審查國家安全方面的任意決定，這標誌著諾姆部長任期內政策的轉變。此前，聯邦法官曾暗示諾姆結束這些保護的舉動可能帶有種族偏見，但白宮對此斷然否認。 [ref:2]

同時，國土安全部週四達到了一個嚴峻的里程碑，標誌著其部分撥款關閉進入第26天。解決預算危機的立法努力再次破裂，參議院預計將在本週休會，而沒有達成協議。民主黨人堅決拒絕批准該機構的新資金，除非對移民和海關執法局（ICE）及海關邊境保護局（CBP）進行重大改革。對這些監督的要求是由今年早些時候兩名美國公民在明尼阿波利斯被聯邦特工槍殺事件催化的，該事件嚴重損害了諾姆部長在國會的聲譽，並促成了她最終被解職。

雖然諾姆計劃留任至3月31日，但向其繼任者、俄克拉荷馬州參議員馬克韋恩·馬林過渡的工作已經開始。參議院國土安全委員會主席蘭德·保羅宣布，馬林的確認聽證會定於3月18日舉行。馬林參議員被總統稱為「MAGA戰士」，預計將面臨艱難的確認過程，因為包括參議院少數黨領袖查克·舒默在內的Opposition領袖已經暗示將投票反對他。這位奧克拉荷馬州參議員的支持者希望他的背景能夠幫助穩定目前因內部士氣問題和無薪員工的財政壓力而搖搖欲墜的機構。 [ref:1]

諾姆部長離開該部門後，計劃領導一項新設立的區域倡議，題為「美洲盾」。本週末，她預計將與總統在邁阿密會見幾位拉丁美洲領導人，簽署《多拉爾憲章》，該憲章專注於瓦解販毒集團和實施跨國移民管制。儘管她因邊境的「輝煌成就」受到官方讚揚，但諾姆部長的離任仍被2.2億美元的納稅人資助的廣告爭議所籠罩。國會調查人員繼續調查指控，稱該廣告活動，其中包括諾姆的顯著出現，是非法使用政府資金進行政治自我推銷。

隨著政府將重點轉向區域安全夥伴關係，當前的國內現實依然是摩擦加劇。像亞特蘭大和新奧爾良這樣的主要國際樞紐的航空安檢等待時間已經激增，因為篩查人員仍在無薪工作，而聯邦緊急事務管理局（FEMA）已警告其災難救濟能力正被推向極限。領導層真空、關於驅逐令保護的司法對峙以及癱瘓的預算，這些因素的匯合繼續定義著國家國內安全基礎設施前所未有的動盪時期。

timeline.title: Trump Administration Scraps Luxury Jet Plans Amid Widening Fallout From Kristi Noem’s Forced Ouster
timeline.title_zh: 特朗普政府取消豪華噴氣機計劃，克里斯蒂·諾姆被迫下台的影響持續擴大
timeline.sub_title: Following the dismissal of DHS Secretary Kristi Noem last week, officials are moving to roll back high-cost projects and address the internal policy friction that led to her removal.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 繼上週國土安全部部長克里斯蒂·諾姆被解僱後，官員們正著手取消高成本項目，並解決導致她被解僱的內部政策摩擦。
timeline.summary:
The internal turmoil within the Department of Homeland Security reached a new peak on Wednesday as the administration began rolling back several high-profile initiatives championed by recently ousted Secretary Kristi Noem. The developments come just six days after President Donald Trump abruptly fired Noem via social media, citing the need for a leadership change amid intensifying friction over border enforcement tactics and departmental spending. While the firing occurred late last week, the full scope of the fallout is only now becoming clear as acting officials and congressional oversight committees begin to dismantle parts of her legacy.

A central development today involves the reported cancellation of a planned acquisition for a Boeing 737 MAX luxury jet, which Noem had intended for official use. Senior DHS officials confirmed that the multi-million-dollar project is being reevaluated as part of a broader effort to address concerns over excessive spending and a lack of transparency that defined Noem’s final months. This move follows a scathing bipartisan inquiry into a $220 million advertising campaign featuring Noem, which critics alleged was a misuse of taxpayer funds designed to burnish her personal image rather than enhance national security.

The friction that led to Noem's dismissal was primarily rooted in her aggressive border policy and a series of controversial immigration enforcement operations. Tensions boiled over following the fatal shootings of two American citizens, Renee Good and Alex Pretti, by federal agents during an operation in Minneapolis. Noem’s subsequent [ref:2, 3, 1] labeling of the victims as "domestic terrorists"—a characterization she refused to retract during two days of heated congressional testimony—alienated not only Democrats but also key Republican allies who viewed the rhetoric as politically toxic. Reports indicate that the President’s decision to replace her was finalized shortly after these hearings, which highlighted deep divisions within the administration regarding the execution of mass deportation plans.

In the wake of her departure, the Department of Homeland Security remains in a precarious state, currently operating under a funding shutdown as Congress remains deadlocked over the agency's budget. Lawmakers have signaled that any new funding agreement will require significant reforms to Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) and the Border Patrol to prevent future incidents like those seen in Minneapolis. Today, civil rights groups issued renewed calls for a fresh vision for the department, urging the administration to move away from the heavy-handed tactics and lack of oversight they associated with Noem’s leadership.

As the transition begins, President Trump has nominated Oklahoma Senator Markwayne Mullin to take the helm of the embattled agency. Mullin, a staunch ally of the President, already faces a difficult confirmation process as he inherits a department plagued by low morale and a restrictive $100,000 spending cap on all contracts that has reportedly hindered disaster relief efforts. Meanwhile, Noem has been transitioned into a new role as "Special Envoy for The Shield of the Americas," a position focused on Western Hemisphere security, though her influence over domestic immigration policy appears to have been effectively neutralized.
timeline.summary_zh:
美國國土安全部（DHS）內部動盪週三達到新高峰，政府開始取消由最近被解職的部長克里斯蒂·諾姆推動的幾項備受矚目的計畫。這些事態發展發生在總統唐納德·特朗普於上週末突然通過社交媒體解僱諾姆之後，理由是在邊境執法策略和部門支出日益加劇的摩擦中，需要領導層變動。儘管解僱發生在上週後期，但隨著代理官員和國會監督委員會開始拆除她遺留的部分政策，其影響的全部範圍才剛剛顯現。
今天的關鍵發展之一是據報導取消了購買波音737 MAX豪華噴氣機的計劃，該計畫是諾姆打算用於官方用途的。國土安全部高級官員證實，正在重新評估這項耗資數百萬美元的項目，作為更廣泛努力的一部分，以解決諾姆擔任部長最後幾個月裡過度支出和缺乏透明度的問題。此前，國會對一項由諾姆參與的2.2億美元廣告活動進行了嚴厲的兩黨質詢，批評者聲稱這是濫用納稅人資金，旨在鞏固她個人形象而非加強國家安全。[ref:2, 3, 1]
導致諾姆被解僱的摩擦主要源于她的強硬邊境政策和一系列有爭議的移民執法行動。在明尼阿波利斯的一次行動中，聯邦特工槍殺了兩名美國公民（Renee Good和Alex Pretti）後，緊張局勢達到了頂點。諾姆隨後將受害者標籤為「國內恐怖分子」——她在兩天的激烈國會聽證會中都拒絕撤回這一說法——這不僅疏遠了民主黨人，也疏遠了關鍵的共和黨盟友，他們認為這種言論具有政治毒性。報導顯示，總統在她退休前不久決定更換她，因為這些聽證會凸顯了政府內部在執行大規模驅逐計劃方面的深刻分歧。
在她離任後，國土安全部仍處於不穩定狀態，由於國會就該機構的預算僵持不下，目前正處於資金關閉狀態。議員們已暗示，任何新的資金協議都將要求對移民及海關執法局（ICE）和邊境巡防局進行重大改革，以防止未來再發生類似明尼阿波利斯事件。今天，人權組織再次呼籲為該部門帶來新的願景，敦促政府擺脫他們與諾姆領導層相關的強硬手段和缺乏監督的狀況。
隨著過渡期的開始，特朗普總統已提名俄克拉荷馬州參議員馬克韋恩·馬林（Markwayne Mullin）接掌這個陷入困境的機構。馬林是總統的堅定盟友，他面臨著艱難的確認程序，因為他繼承了一個士氣低落且所有合同支出都設有10萬美元上限的部門，據報導這已阻礙了災難救援工作。與此同時，諾姆已被調任為「美洲盾特別代表」，負責西半球安全事務，但她對國內移民政策的影響力似乎已被有效消除。
